channel.title: ProbBrain - AI News Terminal
channel.description: AI lab announcements, arXiv papers, GitHub and HuggingFace trending, AI Hacker News, and independent writers - aggregated, deduped, and posted to @ProbBrain on Telegram.

item.title: [OpenAI] How Endava is redesigning software delivery around AI agents
item.description: How Endava is redesigning software delivery around AI agents

item.title: [GitHub Trending] PaddlePaddle/PaddleOCR — Turn any PDF or image document into structured data for your AI. A powerful, lightweight OCR toolkit that bridges the gap between images/PDFs and LLMs. Supports 100+ languages.
item.description: PaddleOCR converts PDFs and images into structured data for AI applications, supporting over 100 languages.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] NVIDIA/cosmos — NVIDIA Cosmos is an open platform of world models, datasets, and tools that enables developers to build Physical AI for robots, autonomous vehicles, smart infrastructure, and more.
item.description: NVIDIA released Cosmos, an open platform of world models, datasets, and tools for building Physical AI for robots and autonomous systems.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] github/copilot-sdk — Multi-platform SDK for integrating GitHub Copilot Agent into apps and services
item.description: GitHub releases multi-platform SDK enabling integration of Copilot Agent into applications and services.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] mvanhorn/last30days-skill — AI agent skill that researches any topic across Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web - then synthesizes a grounded summary
item.description: AI agent skill researches topics across Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web to synthesize grounded summaries.

item.title: [GitHub Trending] sponsors/JuliusBrussee — 🪨 why use many token when few token do trick — Claude Code skill that cuts 65% of tokens by talking like caveman
item.description: Claude Code skill reduces token usage by 65% through simplified language patterns.

item.title: [Hacker News] UK media fails to disclose defence sector links in nearly 60% of cases
item.description: UK media fails to disclose defence sector links in nearly 60% of cases

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Early Detection of Alzheimer's Disease Using Explainable Machine Learning on Clinical Biomarkers: A Multi-Class Classification Study Using the Alzheimer's Disease Neuroimaging Initiative (ADNI) Dataset
item.description: XGBoost model achieves 98.2% accuracy detecting Alzheimer's disease stages using explainable machine learning on clinical biomarkers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Novel Aspects of IEEE SA P3109 Arithmetic Formats for Machine Learning
item.description: IEEE P3109 draft standard defines parameterized binary floating-point formats for machine learning with exception-free operations and formal verification.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Position: Deployed Reinforcement Learning should be Continual
item.description: Deployed reinforcement learning agents should continually learn and adapt post-deployment rather than using static train-then-fix approaches to handle environmental non-stationarity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Pseudospectral Bounds for Transient Amplification in Coupled Gradient Descent
item.description: Pseudospectral theory bounds transient amplification in coupled gradient descent before convergence.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Do Transformers Need Three Projections? Systematic Study of QKV Variants
item.description: Study shows QKV projection sharing in transformers reduces KV cache by 50-96% with minimal accuracy loss, enabling on-device deployment.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Inverse Critical Experiment Design via Gradient Optimization and a Multigroup Attention-Based Neural Network Architecture
item.description: Deep neural networks with multigroup attention pooling optimize nuclear critical experiment designs to maximize neutronic similarity to target reactor technologies.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Self-Distilled Policy Gradient
item.description: SDPG framework combines on-policy self-distillation, verifier advantages, and KL regularization to improve reinforcement learning performance and stability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Bayes-Sufficient Representations in Supervised Learning
item.description: Bayes-sufficient representations preserve only the information necessary for Bayes-optimal predictions, with relevance determined by the loss function.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Unlocking Feature Learning in Gated Delta Networks at Scale
item.description: Researchers extend Maximal Update Parametrization to Gated Delta Networks, enabling stable learning-rate transfer across model widths during language model pre-training.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LiftQuant: Continuous Bit-Width LLM via Dimensional Lifting and Projection
item.description: LiftQuant enables continuous bit-width quantization for LLMs, achieving better compression than fixed-bit methods with 70B models fitting to 2.4 bits on 24GB GPUs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] RUBAS: Rubric-Based Reinforcement Learning for Agent Safety
item.description: RUBAS framework uses rubric-based reinforcement learning to improve LLM agent safety across tool-use, argument, response, and helpfulness dimensions.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] A Goal-Set Characterization of Task Composition in the Boolean Task Algebra
item.description: Research shows optimal task composition in deterministic MDPs depends only on universal and empty base tasks, not logarithmic sets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Spectral Scaling Laws of Muon
item.description: Study identifies power-law scaling in Muon optimizer's momentum matrix singular values across model sizes, with layer-dependent implications for orthonormalization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] LLM Compression with Jointly Optimizing Architectural and Quantization choices
item.description: Differentiable NAS jointly optimizes LLM architecture and quantization, achieving 1.4x faster inference or 6% higher accuracy than sequential compression methods.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] TPA-AD: A Two-Stage Pseudo Anomaly-Guided Method for Bearing Time-Series Anomaly Detection
item.description: Two-stage TPA-AD method detects bearing time-series anomalies using pseudo-anomaly generation and contrastive learning trained on only normal samples.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Adaptive Patching Is Harder Than It Looks For Time-Series Forecasting
item.description: Research shows adaptive patching for time-series Transformers rarely beats well-tuned uniform baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Large Language Models Hack Rewards, and Society
item.description: LLMs exploit loopholes in societal regulations through reward hacking, discovering technically-compliant strategies that defeat regulatory intent.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Stein Kernelized Molecular Dynamics for Active Learning of Interatomic Potentials
item.description: Stein kernelized molecular dynamics method improves active learning of machine learning interatomic potentials through efficient, diversity-aware training data selection.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Building The Ph(ysical)AI Layer Of Machine Intelligence
item.description: Principle-driven foundation models trained on RF data transfer to multiple modalities without fine-tuning, revealing complementary advantages to scale-driven approaches.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Variance Reduction for Heavy-Tailed Monetization Metrics in Ranking Experiments via Post-Stratification
item.description: Post-stratification with CUPED reduces variance in heavy-tailed monetization experiments, achieving equivalent statistical confidence with 45% less traffic.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] dMX: Differentiable Mixed-Precision Assignment for Low-Precision Floating-Point Formats
item.description: dMX introduces differentiable mixed-precision quantization for LLMs, assigning optimal floating-point bit-widths per-layer while maintaining hardware compatibility.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Stationarity-Aware Retrieval-Augmented Time Series Forecasting
item.description: SARAF improves time series forecasting by using stationarity-aware retrieval-augmented generation to balance relevance and diversity in historical data.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Physics-Informed Machine Learning for Short-Term Flood Prediction
item.description: Physics-informed LSTM framework improves short-term flood predictions in data-scarce conditions by incorporating hydrological constraints into the model loss function.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] EvalStop: Using World Feedback to Detect and Correct Reward Overoptimization in Multi-Tenant RLHF Platforms
item.description: EvalStop terminates RLHF fine-tuning jobs after detecting reward model divergence from actual quality metrics to improve GPU utilization.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When Offline Selectors Cannot Beat the Best Single Model: A Diagnostic Study on edX Dropout Prediction
item.description: Diagnostic study reveals offline model selectors fail to beat single best predictor on edX dropout data due to representational ambiguity.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] ADAPTOOD: Uncertainty-Aware Fine-Tuning for Out-of-Distribution ECG Time Series Models
item.description: ADAPTOOD framework uses data uncertainty to quantify distribution shift severity, improving fine-tuning for out-of-distribution ECG time series models.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Smart Transportation Without Neurons -- Fair Metro Network Expansion with Tabular Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Tabular reinforcement learning solves metro network expansion as efficiently as deep RL with 18x fewer training episodes and better interpretability.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] When Autoregressive Consistency Hurts Safety Alignment
item.description: Autoregressive consistency concentrates safety alignment on early tokens, enabling attacks that exploit harmful continuations to bypass LLM safeguards.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] Low-rank Distributional Matrix Completion
item.description: Probabilistic matrix completion using kernel embeddings and Tucker rank for distribution-valued matrices with theoretical guarantees.

item.title: [arXiv cs.LG] KODA: Contrastive Representation Comparison and Alignment for Vision-Language Foundation Models
item.description: KODA is a kernel-based framework for identifying and aligning structural differences in vision-language model representations through contrastive embedding clustering.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] POLARIS: Guiding Small Models to Write Long Stories
item.description: POLARIS method enables 9B-parameter models to write long-form stories on par with much larger models through policy optimization and human guidance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Discourse-Role Labels as Presentation-Time Variables for Context Use in Language Models
item.description: Discourse-role labels shift language models' adoption of incorrect information by 56-84 percentage points, with Instruction and Reference increasing adoption, Example suppressing it.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Computational conceptual history of scientific concepts: From early digital methods to LLMs
item.description: Study traces computational approaches to analyzing scientific concepts from early digital methods through LLMs, examining methodological challenges.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] SaliMory: Orchestrating Cognitive Memory for Conversational Agents
item.description: SaliMory framework trains language models to manage cognitively-structured memory for conversational agents, reducing memory failures and improving personalization rates.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Retrieval Doesn't Help: A Large-Scale Study of Biomedical RAG
item.description: Study finds retrieval-augmented generation provides only 1-2 point improvements for biomedical QA; model capacity, not retriever quality, is the main bottleneck.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Expert-Aware Refusal Steering
item.description: Researchers extend refusal steering techniques to Mixture-of-Experts language models, demonstrating effective suppression of safety refusal behavior through expert-specific routing patterns.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] A Systematic Analysis of Linguistic Features in AI-Generated Text Detection Across Domains and Models
item.description: Empirical analysis of 284 linguistic features across 27 LLMs finds lexical richness measures reliably detect AI-generated text across domains.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] ACAT: A Collaborative Platform for Efficient Aspect-Based Sentiment Dataset Annotation
item.description: ACAT, a web-based platform for aspect-based sentiment analysis annotation, automates alignment of multi-annotator data and computes inter-annotator agreement metrics for training-ready datasets.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Cross-Prompt Generalization in Detecting AI-Generated Fake News Using Interpretable Linguistic Features
item.description: Linguistic feature-based detection of AI-generated fake news generalizes robustly across different generation prompts with AUC scores of 0.988-1.000.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] MM-BizRAG: Rethinking Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation for General Purpose Enterprise Q&A
item.description: MM-BizRAG enhances multimodal retrieval-augmented generation for enterprise documents through explicit structure-aware parsing, outperforming baselines by up to 32%.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Supportive Token Revealing for Fast Diffusion Language Model Decoding
item.description: AXON, a training-free module, improves parallel decoding in diffusion language models by selecting confident tokens that support later denoising, enhancing speed-quality trade-offs.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Can I Take Another Dose? Evaluating LLM Decision-Making Under Temporal Uncertainty in OTC Dosing QA
item.description: Researchers introduce DOSEBENCH benchmark to evaluate LLMs on over-the-counter medication dosing questions, revealing weaknesses in temporal reasoning and safety constraint adherence.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Long Live Fine-Tuning: Task-Specific Transformers Outperform Zero-Shot LLMs for Misinformation Response Classification on Reddit
item.description: Fine-tuned RoBERTa and DistilBERT outperform larger zero-shot LLMs for Reddit misinformation classification with better accuracy and lower cost.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Using Text-Based Causal Inference to Disentangle Factors Influencing Online Review Ratings
item.description: Text-based causal analysis identifies key factors driving online review ratings while accounting for correlations between review aspects.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] LazyAttention: Efficient Retrieval-Augmented Generation with Deferred Positional Encoding
item.description: LazyAttention improves KV caching efficiency through deferred positional encoding, enabling 1.40x faster inference in retrieval-augmented generation applications.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning with Learnable Rank
item.description: LR-LoRA learns per-layer adapter ranks during fine-tuning instead of using fixed ranks across layers.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Noisy memory encoding explains negative polarity illusions
item.description: Lossy memory encoding of complex sentence structure explains why humans accept grammatically incorrect sentences with misplaced negative polarity words.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Deliberate Evolution: Agentic Reasoning for Sample-Efficient Symbolic Regression with LLMs
item.description: New agentic framework improves LLM-based symbolic regression by decoupling generation from search control, outperforming baselines with 40% fewer samples.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] GlossAssist -- A Tool to Simplify Corpus Creation and Study the Effect of NLP Models in Low-Resource Documentation Settings
item.description: GlossAssist automates interlinear glossed text annotation for low-resource languages using active learning that incorporates annotator corrections to improve predictions without retraining.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] DLLG: Dynamic Logit-Level Gating of LLM Experts
item.description: DLLG proposes dynamic logit-level gating to fuse multiple specialized LLMs without token-level labels, outperforming routing and parameter-merging baselines.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] When Clients Stop Following: A Cognitive Conceptualization Diagram-driven Framework for Strategic Counseling
item.description: Researchers propose resistance-aware frameworks to better evaluate LLMs in counseling, addressing evaluation bias where simulated clients unrealistically shift from resistance to compliance.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] Read the Trace, Steer the Path: Trajectory-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Diffusion Language Models
item.description: CAPR algorithm enables efficient reinforcement learning for diffusion language models by extracting block-level supervision signals from denoising traces.

item.title: [arXiv cs.CL] MemoryDocDataSet: A Benchmark for Joint Conversational Memory and Long Document Reasoning
item.description: New benchmark evaluates AI systems combining conversational memory and long-document reasoning using 1,000 QA pairs where 75% require both capabilities.
